content:
1. registry.py - build_index

   Bug: aliases are mapped to themselves instead of to the canonical unit name.

   Code today:
   ```python
   for alias, canonical in ALIASES.items():
       index[alias] = alias
   ```

   Why this is wrong:
   - The module contract says `build_index()` must return a mapping from every alias to the canonical unit name.
   - With the current code, `"meter"` maps to `"meter"` instead of `"m"`, `"centimeter"` maps to `"centimeter"` instead of `"cm"`, etc.

   Concrete impact:
   - Callers using this index to normalise free-text unit names will not get canonical units for aliases.
   - Example: `build_index()["klick"]` returns `"klick"` but must return `"km"`.

   Fix:
   ```python
   for alias, canonical in ALIASES.items():
       index[alias] = canonical
   ```
